stated_goal: Test whether the provider-backed agent can preserve behavior-depth progress while running an explicit final git status and diff-check inspection after all fixes and before submission.
acceptance_criteria: Zero-spend preflight confirms the dedicated runner can call the selected Vertex endpoint without committing sensitive Google identifiers.
The CodeClash run exits successfully under the frozen model, task, runner, and budget.
Raw output is retained under proof/raw with sensitive identifiers and provider-private fields redacted before commit.
The result records provider trajectory, cost, API-call stats, changed files, and full diff scope.
The submitted diff has no helper, scratch, cache, generated, or secret-risk residue.
The submitted diff has no trailing-whitespace or final git diff --check style failure.
The submitted diff includes one behavior-depth improvement beyond board-boundary checks: self-collision prevention.
The trajectory shows final git status --short && git diff --check after whitespace repair and before submission.
No leaderboard, SWE-bench, production, or live-domain claim is made.

evidence.notes: Execution succeeded and strict quality status is clean_behavior_depth_and_final_inspection.
The trajectory records final git status --short && git diff --check returning code 0 after whitespace repair and before submission.
Ephemeral GCE runner installed Docker, CodeClash dependencies, applied the overlay, and ran with explicit global Vertex routing.
Sanitized provider trajectory records the submitted run, API calls, cost, scratch-file deletion, diff-check failure, whitespace repair, final inspection, and submission.
Changed-file set is limited to README_agent.md and main.py; the diff includes self-collision prevention and no added trailing whitespace.
Review separates the final-inspection pass from leaderboard/SWE-bench/production/model-superiority claims and records the empty-blank-line style caveat.
No production or live-domain behavior changed. The only cloud side effect was an ephemeral VM deleted after artifact collection.

falsifiers: The gate must be blocked if the dedicated runner cannot call the selected Vertex endpoint during preflight.
The gate must be blocked if CodeClash cannot run the provider-backed config without leaking credentials.
The gate must be blocked if the run exceeds the frozen $25 ceiling or 45-minute wall-clock ceiling.
The gate must fail if execution succeeds but the submitted diff leaves an unjustified helper, scratch, cache, generated, or secret-risk file.
The gate must fail if execution succeeds but the submitted diff contains trailing whitespace or another final git diff --check failure.
The gate must fail if the trajectory lacks final workspace inspection after all fixes and before submission.
The result must not claim leaderboard, SWE-bench, production, or live-domain verification.
The proof bundle must not commit token, credential JSON, account email, Google Cloud project identifier, or provider-private thought-signature values.
